channel.title: Jira
channel.description: An XML representation of a search request

item.title: [FHIR-55944] Remove MS on MedicationStatement.reasonReference to IPS DiagnosticReport
item.description:
The AU PS IG should not require that AU PS Consumers should display an IPS DiagnosticReport that is referenced in MedicationStatement.reasonReference. It's a step too far for R1.

  • AU PS avoided profiling the DiagnosticReport as this area is still a moving target. It will be technically challenging for implementers
  • I can see no Structures that AU PS derive from that require a DiagnosticReport as a reference in reasonReference. IPS doesn't apply MS on reasonReference, and AU Core doesn't profile DiagnosticReport. 
  • The short on MedicationStatement.reasonReference says 'Condition or observation that supports why the medication is being/was taken.' it does not mention DiagnosticReport.
item.summary: Remove MS on MedicationStatement.reasonReference to IPS DiagnosticReport

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

comments.comment:
Addressing the SHOULD:display aspect - its not a requirement to display. The element has a SHOULD:display obligation. That may or may not be the full resource, or a link, or something else, or no display given that it is SHOULD. There is no AU PS (or IPS or AU Core) requirement to fully display a DiagnosticReport resource or it's representation.

At the moment IPS is a MAY, since it does not place MS or Obligation on that element = MAY for all obligations.

I'm not sure what the issue is, if implementers want to include a DiagnosticReport, that they need to conform to IPS. Why would we prohibit this reference type that is supported in AU Core and allowed in the FHIR standard?

michaelosborne - it boils down to:
1. support for DiagnosticReport in MedicationStatement is an AU Core requirement. 

2. A DiagnosticReport resource when included in an AU PS bundle must be conformant to the AU Core profile (where available) and the IPS profile (where available) - as there isn't yet an AU Core profile this boils down to the IPS profile. So there is a requirement for this in AU PS.

Therefore, until AU Core has a DiagnosticReport profile (this is coming in AU Core R3) its only the IPS profile. Post AU Core R3 it is expected that AU PS R2 will include the corresponding AU PS DiagnosticReport profile that will ensure compliance to both IPS and AU Core.

item.title: [FHIR-54841] the required ValueSet for Condition.clinicalStatus is inadequate to represent clinical status
item.description:
The values in the required value set for Condition.clinicalStatus cannot represent the clinical status of a significant number of conditions based on the pathophysiology and natural history of those conditions. Therefore, it is impossible for many conditions to represent them using the FHIR data model.

As examples:
Conditions such as asthma, migraine headache, sickle cell disease are chronic conditions which have no clinical manifestation for many individuals most of the time. Those individuals are however subject to sudden manifestations of symptoms. Such episodes are often unpredictable. When those manifestations are absent, it would not be correct to assign a value of inactive or in remission or resolved. It would also be inaccurate to refer to the clinical status as active by the usual definition. Any of the above conditions may require ongoing treatment for some individuals even though clinical manifestations are absent and may remain so for months.

A second example of conditions which have clinical status that is not represented by the existing value set is those conditions that are self-limited. There are an extraordinary number of such conditions which are frequently a reason for individuals to seek care. Take as examples a viral respiratory infection, a strep throat, an ankle sprain. An appropriate clinical status would be "active but expected to resolve". Utilizing the existing value set, consider a usually health child whose record contains an encounter at age 6 for strep throat, clinical status active. At age 8, the next encounter in the record shows encounter diagnosis strep throat, clinical status active. The conclusion from a population health analysis of records in this system would conclude that this individual had chronic strep throat.
item.summary: the required ValueSet for Condition.clinicalStatus is inadequate to represent clinical status

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment:
The purpose of Condition.clinicalStatus and Condition.confirmationStatus is to provide important clinical evaluation on the status of the condition to inform clinical decision and effective management of patient's condition.

clinicalStatus value = active (recurrent | relapse) | inactive (remission | resolved) | unknow
Each of these values are relevant and important to inform clinical decisions and management of the patient's condition.
These values are also important for the patient's decision on efforts to management his | her own condition.
A value of remission informs the patient that the condition can return and as such, requires vigilance and continuous adherence to recommended | prescribed management protocol(s).

A value of "not currently manifest", whether it is a child of "active" or sibling of "active" gives the false sense relaxation (especially to some patients who are exhausted | tired of following management protocols day after day, year after year).
It is clinically risky and undesirable.

Emma said 

our EHR has Active, Inactive, Resolved

Patient Care FHIR Conference Call on Feb 26, 2026

Russ requested to add a level 1 code for intermittent, but Stephen said that overlaps with HL7.FHIR.UV.EXTENSIONS\Condition Course Codes - FHIR v5.0.0

Stephen wants to add on-going code (level 2) under active.

Rob said that active and inactive should cover 100% of possibilities, mutually exclusive.

Need to revisit definition of "active"

Level 1 - active - defined as "The subject is currently experiencing the condition or situation, there is evidence of the condition or situation, or considered to be a significant risk."

Is the status the same when patient is having manifestation or not?

Layers:

  • Condition Present or Not?
  • Manifestation Active or Not?
  • Occurrence - Relapse or Remission

On-going vs Present - relates to when symptoms are present

Russ is looking for a value that means "not currently manifest" - to differentiate 

  • Patient says no recent symptoms vs when patient says there are symptoms

Could Observation dates help convey when the manifestation is present or not?

Rob said

I think we need to go back to the first sentence in Score and Usage which states “This resource is used to record detailed information about a condition, problem, diagnosis, or other event, situation, issue, or clinical concept that has risen to a level of concern.”

Regarding some of the examples we have discussed, I’m still concerned about the migraine headaches or the asthma, even if I am having no discernible symptoms when I am being seen today. If I am being monitored or treated because there is a risk of having further symptoms in the future, then the Condition.clinicalStatus would be “active”.

In Person agenda planning:

Plan A - March 26

Plan B - April 2

Also condition adding to the Condition.clinicalStatus set another value = partial remission.

The updated value set will include:
Active

  • ongoing
  • recurrent
  • relapse
  • partial remission (this clinical state still requires ongoing management)
    Inactive
  • remission
  • resolved
    Unknown

For many chronic conditions including asthma, migraine headache, diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, mental illness (such as depression, bipolar disorders) - these conditions can go into partial and full remission if managed effectively. There are many literatures on partial and full remission of these conditions.
Some examples:

  • Asthma remission is characterised by a high level of disease control, including the absence of symptoms and exacerbations, and normalisation or optimisation of lung function with or without ongoing treatment (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9630609/)
  • Various observational studies have identified and classified migraine patterns into 4 main courses: clinical remission, partial clinical remission, persistent and progressive (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9204797/)
  • It’s very common for people to have psychotic symptoms. And so if the mood episode is treated, either major depression or bipolar disorder, those psychotic symptoms usually go into remission (https://www.apa.org/news/podcasts/speaking-of-psychology/serious-mental-illness#:~:text=It's%20very%20common%20for%20people,symptoms%20usually%20go%20into%20remission.)

That said, for blood disorders such as sickle cell disease and thalassaemia, unless treated with gene therapy, and for poorly controlled chronic conditions such as asthma, migraine, depression, bipolar disorders, etc remission is unlikely. These scenarios do call for a review of the Condition.clinicalStatus value set.
Suggest considering addition of "ongoing" to the value set (based on a definition from CDC
"Chronic diseases are defined broadly as conditions that last 1 year or more and require ongoing medical attention or limit activities of daily living or both" (https://www.cdc.gov/chronic-disease/about/index.html)

For the second set of examples: strep sore throat, sprained ankle - when resolved, these conditions should be updated with a Condition.clinicalStatus = resolved.

item.title: [FHIR-55469] Clarification of Scope Regarding National Reusability
item.description:
The front page contains the following sentence: “It provides a foundation for interoperability, ensuring reusability across national projects, European initiatives, and derived HL7 Europe guides.”

In particular, the reference to “ensuring reusability across national projects” appears to be inconsistent with our understanding of the EUCore profiling approach. EUCore profiles seem to primarily focus on cross-border and primary use of data, with less emphasis on national data sharing and secondary use. This is evident, for example, in the requirement for patient and practitioner name, which is appropriate in a cross-border context, while there is no corresponding requirement for identifiers - something that would typically be expected in national cases. Furthermore, in secondary use, names are often not included.

We therefore suggest rephrasing the sentence on the front page, particularly the part referring to national projects, so that it is in closer alignment with the actual profiling.

/ WG in HL7-DK
item.summary: Clarification of Scope Regarding National Reusability

status.description: Decision has been reached that no change will be made to the specification based on the requested change

item.title: [FHIR-55427] Provide reason for cardinality misalignments
item.description:
observationDate[x] and result.value[x] have a remark that list them optional compared to the EU Logical Model.

Please add reasoning for not making them 1.. to match the EU logical model. For example because the result is still in progress and thus you cannot know the result yet. Another reason might be that you had to use extension(value-r5) because the result was an Attachment hence you cannot use Observation.value[x]
item.summary: Provide reason for cardinality misalignments

item.title: [FHIR-55545] Composition: Location of .category:ehds-category
item.description:
https://hl7.eu/fhir/base/2.0.0-ballot/StructureDefinition-composition-eu-core-definitions.html#Composition.category:ehds-category

This slice and related coding would potentially be more suitable in Composition.type, where FHIRDocumentTypeCodes are preferred Binding. The .category element is used in the HL7 Europe Laboratory IG to depict Laboratory specialties, so is already a more granular level than here. Please align between IGs
item.summary: Composition: Location of .category:ehds-category

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: According to decision on https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55541 cateory slice has been removed from the DiagnostiReport; for the same reason we shoudl do the same here.

I do not disagree about the need of using category for coarse filtering of documents, my doubt is to define in this version of this guide a slice when the actual value set to be used is not ready/mature.

Anything prevents to use "that" category even if a slice is not specified in the IG

I agree with Patrick - I think if we want to keep EHDS category here we should have multiple slices. 

On the DocRef.Type (and Composition.Type) field, this one should be left to represent clinical precision (e.g. specific LOINC codes). I don't think we should put a generic category there. 

Note: Category is useful in DocRef searches to differentiate the "category" of document. So it should be a short list of search facets. We are working through this on the API side: Document Search and Priority Category Differentiation · Issue #11 · euridice-org/eu-health-data-api

Current thinking: Type is clinical precision (LOINC), Category is search differentiation (local codeset representing priority categories, or some other short list of generic codes)

Note: the search for document data is based on DocumentReference, not Composition (though, they're linked. But I don't think it's a hard link)

As a Composition has multiple categories it can be used to represent both:
priority category & lab specialities.

I personally see a usage for having the priority category on Composition (and also on DocumentReference) to query a FHIR endpoint for all relevant documents of a priority category

item.title: [FHIR-55516] Use of periodOfLife on a DateTime Element
item.description:
In the Immunization profile, periodOfLife is used on the occurrenceDateTime element. While this is technically possible, it is not ideal semantically: the extension represents a life period, i.e., a timespan, not a specific point in time.
Life phases (“childhood,” “school age,” etc.) are conceptually better expressed using a Range type. The German TC FHIR has already discussed this; therefore, the life‑phase extension is implemented as Type Range in Germany.

If the current modeling is intentional, a brief explanation of the underlying design rationale would be helpful.
item.summary: Use of periodOfLife on a DateTime Element

status.description: All of the agreed changes have been applied to the specification

comments.comment: Created CP for the extension package https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55976

Discussed in 20.02.2026 Base/Core call (spoiler alert - no resolution yet).

The issue is a combination of two topics

1) What is the context of use for this extension? Should it be narrower or broader?

 

Currently:

Since Germany is already using it on Range, we would not restrict, but rather add it on Range. We also don't feel comfortable reducing the data types the extension applies to, because we don't have enough information about the actual use yet.

Range.low and Range.high would allow multiple CodeableConcepts merged into one longer period. When on Range directly, low and high would be empty and only extension value would be present.

Action item: Open issue in Extensions package for this. 

 

2) How to make the extension explicit in the profiles: Allergy Intolerance (EU core), Condition (EU core), Immunization (EU core), Procedure (EU core)

Would we make it explicit for all data types where it's possible to use? Would we pick one data type where we make it explicit (please note that Immunization only allows dateTime out of the current options)? Or we would not make it explicit at all.

This is to be discussed further.

adamzkover

1) But string is absolutely not structured. So having these options only, i would prefer using DateTime.

2) The value in the extension is a codeableconcept -> https://simplifier.net/basisprofil-de-r4/extensionlebensphase 

Our wish: if range is available the extension should be applied there.

Using Immunization as an example:

Immunization.occurrence is either string or dateTime.

Several aspects of the "period of life" extension are relevant here.

1. Which data types are the extension allowed on?

In the case of Immunization, string could be a better alternative. DateTime is "a specific point in time"; the string description is closer to a period-of-life concept.

Can the allowed types be extended to allow the extension to be used on strings?

2. What data types can the extension value have?

"Therefore, the life‑phase extension is implemented as Type Range in Germany." – Does this mean that the extension value is a Range in the German implementation, instead of CodeableConcept? In this case, I agree that Range is better than a single dateTime, but CodeableConcept sounds even better than Range for the purpose of representing a period of life.
That is correct, but this comment is more general. The position of the extension in the profiles allergy intolerance, condition and procedure should be range in onset and abatement too
If the answer is satisfactory please consider to retract the comment, otherwise it will be voted with the block 2

item.title: [FHIR-55023] Remove Observation.organizer element if List is added as an option for DiagnosticReport.result
item.description: If the List resource is added as an option for DiagnosticReport.result (https://jira.hl7.org/browse/FHIR-55019) then this will serve the purpose of the "grouper" for laboratory (and other diagnostic) panels, and in that case Observation.organizer would no longer be needed (and retaining it would likely result in further confusion).  The 'organizer' element was only recently added in the last ballot and was "trial use" until this first R6 normative ballot, so the removal of this element should not be considered as a breaking change to the normative resource.  It's purpose would be superseded by the new recommendation to use the List resource for this purpose.
item.summary: Remove Observation.organizer element if List is added as an option for DiagnosticReport.result

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

item.title: [FHIR-54887] Several changes to Observation based on the outcome around the Observation grouping / Panel Representation discussion
item.description:
the value set for https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/observation-definitions.html#Observation.code - which is currently https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/valueset-observation-codes-observationorboth.html (example binding only) should be updated or a rule should be added to allow use of LOINC order or both, when https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/observation-definitions.html#Observation.organizer = true

If https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/observation-definitions.html#Observation.organizer is removed, then this is mute

#2: https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/observation.html#obsgrouping and https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/observation.html#gr-result - this text needs to be updated - if not here, then in the Diagnostics Module page

#3 For grouping related observations such as for a "panel" or "battery". In this case the Observation.code represents the "panel" code, typically Observation.value[x] is not present, and the set of member Observations are listed in Observation.hasMember. This structure permits nested grouping when used with DiagnosticReport (e.g. complex micro isolate and sensitivities report) as described in the Diagnostics Module - the text needs to be adjusted depending on grouping outcome - at minimum, a sentence needs to be added to indicate Observation.organizer = true in this case
item.summary: Several changes to Observation based on the outcome around the Observation grouping / Panel Representation discussion

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

item.title: [FHIR-54803] Text summaries for vital signs mix category and code requirements
item.description:
There are remainders of the previous approach in the textual summaries for the OxSat profile and the BP profile:

OxSat has this text:

  • a fixed Observation.category.coding.code= '2708-6'

This should be:

  • a fixed Observation.code.coding.code= '2708-6'

==

BP has this:

  • a fixed Observation.category.coding.system='http ://loinc.org'

This should be:

  • a fixed Observation.code.coding.system='http ://loinc.org'

==

Also the text structure around these statements need to be reworked.

==

Also the other vital sign profiles should be checked for such remainders of the previous approach.
item.summary: Text summaries for vital signs mix category and code requirements

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

item.title: [FHIR-54521] Inconsistency in Observationvitalsignsbase
item.description:
Within the "Text Summary" point 7 ("When using a panel code") says that:

  • a LOINC code in Observation.code.coding.code which has an extensible binding to:
    • Vital Signs Units value set.

Connected with that statement are three mistakes:

  • Instead of Observation.code.coding.code it should be Observation.component.code.coding.code
  • Instead of Vital Signs Units it should be Vital Signs
  • Most of all, in contrast to the statement in point 7 of "Text Summary" the profile does not specify a value set binding at all for https://hl7.org/fhir/6.0.0-ballot4/vitalsigns-definitions.html#vitalsigns.Observation.component.code
item.summary: Inconsistency in Observationvitalsignsbase

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

item.title: [FHIR-54508] Apparent contradiction in section 10.1.17 regarding obligation of having both diastolic and systolic components
item.description: TThere's an apparent contradiction in section 10.1.17 regarding obligation of having both diastolic and systolic components. The last sentence of the third bullet states it is mandatory to have both components. However the table below states "...contains at least one component (systolic and/or diastolic)...". These statements should be consistent with each other and with the formal requirements of the respective profile (which I understand requires both components)
item.summary: Apparent contradiction in section 10.1.17 regarding obligation of having both diastolic and systolic components

item.title: [FHIR-53902] Vital signs search examples inconsistent with interoperability code placement
item.description:
The profile specifies that interoperability LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4, 9279-1, 85354-9) now reside in the category element rather than code. However, the search examples still demonstrate searching by code parameter with these LOINCs:

GET [base]/Observation?patient=[id]&code[vital sign LOINC\{,LOINC2,LOINC3,...}]

GET [base]/Observation?patient=1186747&code=8867-4

...

 Suggested change: Update all examples to search by category when using interoperability LOINC codes, or clarify the intended search behavior given the new element placement.
item.summary: Vital signs search examples inconsistent with interoperability code placement

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment:
Questions for vital signs SMEs:

Below are some hypothetical searches:

  1. Find all vital signs: category=vital-signs
  2. Find all vital signs: category=85353-1
  3. Find all respiratory rate vital signs method 1: category=vital-signs,9279-1
  4. Find all respiratory rate vital signs method 2: category=9279-1  
  5. Find vital signs specifically related to Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood: category=2708-6
  6. Find vital signs specifically related to Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry method 1:  category=2708-6&code=59408-5
  7. Find vital signs specifically related to Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry method 2:  code=59408-5

 

Are searches #1 and #2 effectively the same, given that 85353-1 is the interoperability category for the overall panel? Or is #2 potentially a subset?

Are searches #3 and #4 both valid and equivalent?

Is search #7 valid in that it searches for the more specific code without the categorization? Note: If so none of the example search requests in 10.1.19 are actually wrong as each of the "Interoperability Category" (LOINC) codes are present in the .code value set bindings.

It may be warranted to include some new examples of finding vital signs just by category code, or the examples reworked to specifically use the category for search rather than just the code, if that is the preferred pattern.

item.title: [FHIR-55977] Support Interval<System.Time>
item.description:
The current FHIR Type Mapping does not call out the specific case of how an Interval of System.Time is mapped. Because Period only supports dateTime, we need to specify how dateTime is used to round trip a System.Time value.

Propose that we use the minimum date value and round-trip using that. For example:

T10 would be represented in FHIR as @0001-01-01T10

and then when coming back to a System.Time, @0001-01-01T10 would be converted to a System.Time based on the cqlType specifier indicating that the value is a System.Time, rather than a DateTime.

See the Zulip discussion here:

https://chat.fhir.org/#narrow/channel/179220-cql/topic/FHIR.20Representation.20of.20CqlInterval.3CCqlTime.3E/with/574482132
item.summary: Support Interval<System.Time>

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

Add a time-interval-valued element to the complete mapping example

item.title: [FHIR-54410] Include category concepts in Detailed Ethnicity value set
item.description:
Update Detailed Ethnicity (2.16.840.1.114222.4.11.877) value set to include the category level concepts of 2135-2 Hispanic or Latino and 2186-5 Not Hispanic or Latino

 

Note: the categories are necessary to support computable roll-up from detailed ethnicity (and race) concepts to the categories. The Detailed Race value set includes a member value set with all detailed race concepts, and the five category level concepts. Race Value Set (2.16.840.1.113883.1.11.14914)
item.summary: Include category concepts in Detailed Ethnicity value set

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: This aligns with the way we built the race sets

I think we should update as proposed: Update Detailed Ethnicity (https://vsac.nlm.nih.gov/valueset/2.16.840.1.113883.4.642.40.2.48.1/expansion) value set to include the category level concepts of 2135-2 Hispanic or Latino and 2186-5 Not Hispanic or Latino. Note that the set bound in the extension (https://vsac.nlm.nih.gov/valueset/2.16.840.1.113883.4.642.40.2.48.1/expansion) contains the Detailed Ethnicity set along with DataAbsentReason, and NullFlavor sets
nm
I will fight this change, because there is no reason to change the extension. It has been this way for 10 yrs.

item.title: [FHIR-55972] DiagnosticReport constraint prevents inclusion of patient history
item.description:
Constraint dgr-1 states 

When a Composition is referenced in `DiagnosticReport.composition`, all Observation resources referenced in `Composition.entry` must also be referenced in `DiagnosticReport.result` or in the `Observation.hasMember` references.

This requires all observations in the composition to be present in the result field. This becomes strange if the Composition refers to historical data (e.g. vitals) that are not a result of the report. Please remove the constraint.
item.summary: DiagnosticReport constraint prevents inclusion of patient history

item.title: [FHIR-55922] Device ConformsTo context updates
item.description:
The device-conformsTo extension appears to be the definition to use instead of a related cross-version definition.

The same structure appears in both Device and DeviceDefinition, but the extension currently only lists Device as a valid context target.

Please expand the context to include DeviceDefinition.

Also, please add http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/version-specific-use tags so that the extension is only valid in versions where the elements do not exist.

As a note, both the extension and DeviceDefinition.conformsTo have a source element, while Device.conformsTo does not. I am not sure if that is intentional or an oversight.
item.summary: Device ConformsTo context updates

item.title: [FHIR-55958] Add example workflow showing the use of verbal order
item.description:
With the resolution of FHIR-34207, we should also provide some guidance on how undocumented or a posteriori documentation of orders can happen.

This can include a generic example (or several) of how to document such orders

  • before or after fulfillment has started,
  • for lab, pharmacy or others,
  • including the need to actaully create a filler order vs no such need
  • ...

Not sure if this would be a new pattern, but perhaps it fits better into order initiation.

item.title: [FHIR-54425] Last name and first name
item.description:
The cardinality indicates the data item is required. There is no reason that an EHR (of an ambulatory practice) would not have a patient name. Therefore, I think the IG Status needs to be: Should be required, Cannot be absent.  

Cardinality 1..1

Data absent reason = No

Must support = Yes
item.summary: Last name and first name

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
We recommend not persuasive for the following reasons:

  • There may be use cases such as for hospitals (e.g., non-CoC hospitals without cancer registries to also implement the IG), not only ambulatory facilities. 
  • In some countries, people don't always have both first and last name
  • We believe it is best to follow the parent profile as closely as possible to promote interoperability
  • There may be instances where the name is not available at the time the report is generated
  • Since the element is Must Support, it will always be included in the report if it's in the patient record.
This issue was entered on behalf of a NAACCR community member.

item.title: [FHIR-55903] Update sections in sharing content and tracking progress
item.description:
This adds to FHIR-55629:

After changing the page "sharing content", the sections should be adjusted to:

      1. Sharing Inputs
        to
      2. Sharing inputs at time of ordering
        1. Requesting Additional Information
          to
      2. Sharing additional information about a clinical order
        and
        1. Provided by placer unsolicited
          and
        2. Request for additional information by fulfiller
      1. Sharing Intermediate Outputs
        to
      2. Sharing outcomes of clinical orders
        1. Preliminary Results, Addenda, and Updates with FHIR Servers
          to
        2. Preliminary or Intermediate Results, Addenda, and Updates with FHIR Servers

In the page tracking progress, change
In general progress is tracked via *Task.status, **Task.businessStatus, and **Task.statusReason*.
to
Clinical orders can have varying life-cycles. In general progress is tracked via `Task.status`, `Task.businessStatus`, and `Task.statusReason`. In cases where the progress can be ''coded'' systems should use the `.businessStatus` element in the Coordination Task to represent that progress status e.g. `collection-complete` or `mandatory-program-complete`.

If further detail on progress is needed, the mechanism depends on whether there is a need to persist and exchange the progress, or whether the stakeholders can consult the relevant resources to determine progress from those resources.

In many cases the fulfillment of clinical orders is a multi-step process that can involve the occurrence of several instances of services or events. The following are two mechanisms for persisting and exchanging the information about how many instances have been completed.
item.summary: Update sections in sharing content and tracking progress

item.title: [FHIR-55366] Why is scoring a modifier
item.description: The modifier reason says it's needed to understand the measure, but it's optional, and it's also bound to an extensible value set.  Modifier doesn't mean 'important' it means that if you interpret this instance ignoring the element, you'll understand it correctly.  (E.g. a patient isn't really a patient if they're not active, a prescription isn't really a prescription if it's entered in error.)  This doesn't seem to meet that bar.  Same issue for dataUpdateType and improvementNotation
item.summary: Why is scoring a modifier

status.description: There is agreement that a change of some sort needs to be made in response to the requested change

comments.comment: 2/25/26 subgroup call – Bryn has confirmed with submitter that proposed resolution is acceptable.

"isModifier" isn't about "is this safe to ignore?", it's "does ignoring this cause an incorrect interpretation of the instance".  ServiceRequest.subject would be a very bad thing to ignore, however it's not a modifier - because ignoring it doesn't cause an incorrect interpretation".  "Someone/something unknown has an order for a lab test" is still an accurate interpretation.  However, status is a modifier because "Jane Smith has an order for a lab result with unknown status" and "This record of a lab test order for Jane Smith was never correct" (i.e. 'entered-in-error') do lead to different (and incorrect) understandings.

bryn.rhodes lloyd - Discussed and decided that scoring and improvementNotation should probably have the Is Modifier property set to false given that we were able to think of use cases where they did not apply and we could safely ignore the value.  dataUpdateType is a little clear, but it does not change the state of the resource, so that also might need the Is Modifier set to false.
Unfortunately not.  I have a conflicting appointment I can't move.  I could possibly do the 13th from 1-2.
lloyd Will you be able to join the CQI call on Feb. 6 (Friday), 1-3pm ET?

Jan 26 - These fall in the category of expected workflow.  You need them to interpret what you are looking at.  Would like to get Bryn's understanding if why we made MeasureReport an Is Modifier, and Measure does not have that.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: Agree that scoring, dataUpdateType and improvementNotation do not meet the definition for Is Modifier.  We will remove Is Modifier and remove the reason for all 3 elements.

item.title: [FHIR-54428] SSN
item.description: I am concerned about the Data Absent Reason, because fewer practitioners are collecting SSN.  If it is in the EHR, they have it; if it isn't in the EHR, they won't have. The data item is not required; and I think it will be a burden for providers to indicate why it is not there.
item.summary: SSN

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
We recommend not persuasive for the following reasons:

  • Data absent reason on all identifiers is inherited from US Public Health Library (USPHL) Profiles so we can't remove it (unless we change the patient profile to no longer have USPHL patient profile as its parent. If we removed the USPHL profile as the parent, we would need to conform instead to the US Core Patient profile, which is missing many of important extensions and constraints needed for cancer registry reporting. 
  • All implementers who conform to the USPHL will already be implementing it, so we don't see it as an added burden.
  • Data absent reason is not required; the specification says that it should be provided when the data are not available. 
  • We do not believe that allowing a data absent reason will put an extra burden on providers. Systems likely have a rules-based method to fill the data absent reason when applicable.
  • We believe allowing it will actually lessen, not increase, the burden on the vendors, because it will enable to the system to send the report without the messing data causing an error.
This issue was entered on behalf of a NAACCR community member.

item.description: Recommendation: If, when populated, include number of years (15) and/or range (for example, from 1980 -1995) when the patient was active in that occupation (i.e., include "period" (start and end") or just range.

An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
Recommend not persuasive because there already is a period element for the occupation, described as: 

The period.start asserts when the usual occupation began. NOTE: If the period.start element is missing, the start of the usual occupation is not known. The period.end asserts when the usual occupation ended. If usual occupation is current, period.end should be omitted.

Note: there is no mapping to any NAACCR data element for period start or end dates.

item.title: [FHIR-55905] Add
item.description:
Implementers have suggested the need for a "Mark complete" task similar to cancellation request task. After further investigation this was not needed in that case, and the notion of sharing the updated Coordination task was sufficient.
This indicates that some guidance may be useful.

1. (at least) in the Prescription and Dispense with single Dispense task, we should include in the diagram POST Task back to EHR - it is important to let implementers know that this is the normal mechanism to exchange overall status.

2. provide some guidance in the "events, requests and tasks" page? Or better - in Core concepts, explaining that orders can be cancelled by placer or be requested to be cancelled by filler, and marked complete by placer, e.g. upon noticing the fulfillment has been done.

3. consider the need to describe a Completion request task - and if that makes sense as a concept, add some guidance when NOT to do it
item.summary: Add

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: Sorry, title should be "Add guidance on marking order "complete""

item.title: [FHIR-54381] Laboratory Test Results section
item.description: Is there a Laboratory Test Date (under the Resource Profile: US Core Laboratory Results Observation Profile) that can be added, and required if lab results exist? For example, PSA can change during treatment so we need to know if the PSA value is from the diagnosis or not.
item.summary: Laboratory Test Results section

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
There already is a laboratory test date in the US Core Laboratory Results Observation Profile. It is Must Support and cardinality of 0..1. Must support means it will always be included in the report if the date is recorded in the EHR. But since it is a cardinality of 0..1, if the date is not available in the EHR, the report can still be sent. 

Recommend not persuasive for the following reasons:

  • While we agree that having this date is very important, we don't want reports to be rejected if it's missing. 
  • Most labs will have these dates, but in the rare case that they are missing, it is still better to know that than have the entire report rejected. 
  • We believe it is important to align with the US Core parent profile as much as possible, and this requirement would be a deviation from that.
This issue was entered on behalf of a NAACCR community member.

item.title: [FHIR-54120] Consent Administrator Access Management
item.description:
For the next version we should address how to manage at scale whether a data source that has a patient's data can access only some of a patient's consent rules to determine who they can or cannot further share that patient's information.

Scenarios to consider are whether a data source that can receive some information on a patient can see consents that involve the data that they cannot receive.  Currently the focus is more on accessing all consent rules or none.  Other scenarios are where one or more providers for a patient can share information with a particular party, but other providers cannot share with that particular party.  While the rule itself can include that logic, particularly when a patient's consent rules are managed in a distributed fashion sharing too many consent rules becomes more challenging.
item.summary: Consent Administrator Access Management

status.description: An change proposal has been fully categorized and is ready to be actioned

comments.comment: Will accept writeups against the diagram from Hans to explain the issue

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: We will add a section that details a note to implementers that a concern that we haven't addressed is that a data source that has a patient's data does not automatically get access to all of the patient's consents. They should only receive consents that deal with the data that the source is concerned with. Since the guide is concerned with the sharing of consent instances across consent registries, the actual rules around sharing of those can get tricky.

item.title: [FHIR-54131] Constraint vs. Exception
item.description: While this mostly is a FHIR Core topic (a separate Jira will be submitted to FHIR R6), the provision attribute uses to different terms raising questions on what it actually means.  In one definition/description the term "constraint" is used and the other "exception".  Those are two different concepts.  Which definition is actually used in this profile?
item.summary: Constraint vs. Exception

item.title: [FHIR-54132] Consent.policyRule reference
item.description: The .provision is Must Support and the definition indicates that this is a constraint (or exception) to the Consent.policyRule.  Yet the .policyRule is 0..1 in the base, but not stated as a Must Support in the profile.  How can a provision be defined without a policyRule in place (if the definition is accurate), thus why is policyRule not Must Support?
item.summary: Consent.policyRule reference

item.title: [FHIR-54383] Develop a new (smaller) value set for "evidenceType" which maps to NAACCR Diagnostic Confirmation
item.description: 7.6.1 Resource Profile: Central Cancer Registry Reporting Primary Cancer Condition.  The value set for Cancer Disease Status Evidence Type Value Set - minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCODE) Implementation Guide v4.0.0 is very large and seems like it has a lot of concepts not needed (more granular) by CCRs. This element maps to NAACCR Diagnostic Confirmation, so if the current value set is used, it will make mapping burdensome. Also need to consider whether they all clearly map to a single NAACCR value or if there is some ambiguity. Recommend SMEs develop a narrower value set (from SNOMED) that is more closely aligned with NAACCR values and use that.
item.summary: Develop a new (smaller) value set for "evidenceType" which maps to NAACCR Diagnostic Confirmation

status.description: Proposed changes have been submitted and are awaiting review from the Terminology Curator to ensure there are no major issues with the proposal. An automated IG build has also been initiated to help the submitter with content review, but it is not a requirement for submitters to review the IG build output.

comments.comment:
Recommend not persuasive for the following reasons:

  • It should not be too difficult to aggregate/rollup these codes on the registry side and would allow for more consistency if the registries take them as is from the EHRs rather than ask the EHRs to implement the aggregation on their side. 
  • The code rollup should not be too difficult, as many of the codes can be mapped to their parents to facilitate the mapping to NAACCR codes
  • EHRs may already have these codes in their systems, but mapping to the higher level concepts would be varied, so better to handle it on receiving side.
This issue was entered on behalf of a NAACCR community member.

item.title: [FHIR-54681] IHE-PCF
item.description:
Given that this IG is not covering enforcement. It might be useful to point out that IHE-PCF does include enforcement that leverages OAuth. Although IHE did not mention HL7 SMART App Launch IG on OAuth, it is explicitly designed to be compatible with it. 
https://profiles.ihe.net/ITI/PCF/volume-1.html#actors-and-transactions
item.summary: IHE-PCF

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
We refer to the IHE-PCF guide in a number of places but we will be more explicit throughout the guide to reference:

  • actors
  • our operations vs the transaction
  • the profiles being "US-Core-ized" flavours of the IHE-PCF profiles
  • etc.

We will also call out the OAuth point in this issue: IHE-PCF does include enforcement that leverages OAuth. Although IHE did not mention HL7 SMART App Launch IG on OAuth, it is explicitly designed to be compatible with it.

item.title: [FHIR-54963] Fix 5Ws mappings when only backbone element are displayed
item.description:
The mappings for name - do not include the Device.name and it is difficult to understand the context of these elements in 5Ws.

 

       type FiveWs.what[x]
        display FiveWs.status

 

 name C 0..* BackboneElement The name or names of the device as known to the manufacturer and/or patient
 
 value Σ 1..1 string The term that names the device
 type Σ 1..1 CodeableConcept registered-name | user-friendly-name | patient-reported-name
Binding: Device Name Type (Extensible)
 display ?! Σ C 0..1 boolean The preferred device name

 

Will also submit a ticket to FHIR-I to find out why the tooling does not display them.
item.summary: Fix 5Ws mappings when only backbone element are displayed

status.description: The change proposal is on hold while awaiting input from an individual or group.

comments.comment:
Current - it is now missing altogether in CI Build - where it was partially represented.  Need to determine if there is something missing in the mapping elements of structured definition.

Device administrative.device
    identifier FiveWs.identifier
    udiCarrier FiveWs.identifier
        deviceIdentifier FiveWs.what[x]
    status FiveWs.status
    availabilityStatus FiveWs.status
    biologicalSourceEvent FiveWs.what[x]
    manufacturer FiveWs.what[x]
    manufactureDate FiveWs.what[x]
    expirationDate FiveWs.what[x]
    lotNumber FiveWs.what[x]
    serialNumber FiveWs.what[x]
        type FiveWs.what[x]
        display FiveWs.status
    modelNumber FiveWs.what[x]
    partNumber FiveWs.what[x]
        component FiveWs.what[x]
        installDate FiveWs.what[x]
    additive FiveWs.what[x]
        type FiveWs.what[x]
        quantity FiveWs.what[x]
    location FiveWs.where[x]

 

In SD: 

<element id="Device.name">
      <extension url="http://hl7.org/fhir/build/StructureDefinition/svg">
        <valueCode value="400,0"/>
      </extension>
      <path value="Device.name"/>
      <short value="The name or names of the device as known to the manufacturer and/or patient"/>
      <definition value="This represents the manufacturer's name of the device as provided by the device, from a UDI label, or by a person describing the Device.  This typically would be used when a person provides the name(s) or when the device represents one of the names available from DeviceDefinition."/>
      <min value="0"/>
      <max value="*"/>
      <type>
        <code value="BackboneElement"/>
      </type>
      <condition value="dev-1"/>
      <mapping>
        <identity value="interface"/>
        <map value="Participant.name"/>
      </mapping>
    </element>
    <element id="Device.name.value">
      <path value="Device.name.value"/>
      <short value="The term that names the device"/>
      <definition value="The actual name that identifies the device."/>
      <alias value="Σ"/>
      <min value="1"/>
      <max value="1"/>
      <type>
        <code value="string"/>
      </type>
      <isSummary value="true"/>
    </element>
    <element id="Device.name.type">
      <extension url="http://hl7.org/fhir/build/StructureDefinition/committee-notes">
        <valueString value="W5 note what = what.productidentification."/>
      </extension>
      <path value="Device.name.type"/>
      <short value="registered-name | user-friendly-name | patient-reported-name"/>
      <definition value="Indicates the kind of name. RegisteredName | UserFriendlyName | PatientReportedName."/>
      <comment value="The implementation or jurisdiction must clarify which name is to be used as the registered-name. As a result a device definition may have multiple names that are the same, but then used for different purposes per the name type and jurisdictional guidance."/>
      <min value="1"/>
      <max value="1"/>
      <type>
        <code value="CodeableConcept"/>
      </type>
      <isSummary value="true"/>
      <binding>
        <extension url="http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/elementdefinition-bindingName">
          <valueString value="DeviceNameType"/>
        </extension>
        <strength value="extensible"/>
        <description value="The type of name the device is referred by."/>
        <valueSet value="http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/device-nametype"/>
      </binding>
      <mapping>
        <identity value="w5"/>
        <map value="FiveWs.what[x]"/>
      </mapping>
      <mapping>
        <identity value="rim"/>
        <map value=".playedRole[typeCode=MANU].code"/>
      </mapping>
    </element>
    <element id="Device.name.display">
      <extension url="http://hl7.org/fhir/build/StructureDefinition/committee-notes">
        <valueString value="W5 note what = what.productidentification."/>
      </extension>
      <path value="Device.name.display"/>
      <short value="The preferred device name"/>
      <definition value="Indicates the default or preferred name to be displayed."/>
      <min value="0"/>
      <max value="1"/>
      <type>
        <code value="boolean"/>
      </type>
      <condition value="dev-1"/>
      <isModifier value="true"/>
      <isModifierReason value="This element is labelled as a modifier because it is a status element that can indicate that that a name is preferred."/>
      <isSummary value="true"/>
      <mapping>
        <identity value="w5"/>
        <map value="FiveWs.status"/>
      </mapping>
      <mapping>
        <identity value="rim"/>
        <map value="statusCode"/>
      </mapping>
    </element>

 

NOTE: device-participant-mapping-exceptions.xml includes exception for name - could that be the reason it is not showing up in the 5Ws?

<divergentElement patternPath="Participant.name" resourcePath="Device.name">
        <upperCardinality _pattern="1" _resource="*" reason="Unknown"/>
        <missingTypes _pattern="string" reason="Unknown"/>
        <extraTypes _resource="BackboneElement" reason="Unknown"/>
        <summary _pattern="true" _resource="false" reason="Unknown"/>
        <shortUnmatched reason="Unknown">
            <_pattern value="A name for the device"/>
            <resource value="The name or names of the device as known to the manufacturer and/or patient"/>
        </shortUnmatched>
        <definitionUnmatched reason="Unknown">
            <_pattern value="Description of the device as presented to a consumer while searching."/>
            <resource value="This represents the manufacturer's name of the device as provided by the device, from a UDI label, or by a person describing the Device.  This typically would be used when a person provides the name(s) or when the device represents one of the names available from DeviceDefinition."/>
        </definitionUnmatched>
    </divergentElement>

Reopening a ballot requires adhering to HL7 rules around revisiting decisions. (Advance notice and 2/3 majority vote in favor of revisiting is generally required.) Include a reference to the minutes of the decision to revisit in the comments as well as rationale for re-opening the item.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Copy the IN parameters 'reporter' and 'reporterResource' on $evaluate to $care-gaps.

Remove the IN parameters 'organization' on $care-gaps, and the parameter 'organizationResource' on $collect-data and $care-gaps.

In $evaluate, $care-gaps, and $collect-data
Update the 'reporterResource' description's first sentence to:

"The provider for which the report will be run, provided as a Practitioner, PractitionerRole, or Organization resource."

Make 'reporterResource' type Organization or Practitioner or PractitionerRole
